description: Common fraud and scam patterns documented by cybersecurity and consumer-protection authorities. Paraphrased from public reports; NOT verbatim from any copyrighted work.

entries.description: Building a long romantic or friendship relationship online to eventually pressure the target into fraudulent crypto or investment schemes.
entries.source: Interpol, Global Financial Fraud Alert (2022); FBI IC3 2022 Internet Crime Report. Paraphrased.

entries.description: Threatening to release intimate images or recordings of the target unless they pay money or comply with further demands.
Pretending to be a technology company or government agency to gain remote access to the target's device or extract payment for fake repairs.
entries.source: FTC consumer alerts; CISA advisory AA21-201A. Paraphrased.

entries.description: Cultivating a fake romantic relationship online to extract money through a series of escalating financial crises or 'loans'.
keywords: I love you and need your help
entries.source: FBI 2023 Internet Crime Report; FTC romance scam report. Paraphrased.

entries.description: Promising a large reward in exchange for a small upfront payment, after which the reward never materialises.
Sending fraudulent communications that appear to come from a trusted institution to steal credentials, financial data, or install malware.
entries.source: CISA; Anti-Phishing Working Group (APWG) reports. Paraphrased.

entries.description: Pretending to be a government authority, law enforcement, or a relative in distress to coerce immediate payment or personal data.
